mathml_representations_list:
<mstyle id="custom-group-4" class="custom-group"><mi id="mi_130">d</mi>
        <mo stretchy="false">(</mo>
        <mo>∂</mo>
        <mi mathvariant="normal" id="mi_131">Ω</mi>
        </mstyle>

concepts_list.name: u_velocity
concepts_list.justification: Appears multiple times as a bold variable in momentum and continuity equations, denoted as u in the context of fluid motion.

concepts_list.name: w_test_function
concepts_list.description: Test function used in the variational formulation
concepts_list.justification: Explicitly described as a test function in H^1(Ω) that vanishes at Dirichlet boundaries, appearing in integral formulations.

concepts_list.name: rho_density
concepts_list.justification: Used in both momentum and continuity equations, represented as ρ and associated with mass and momentum conservation.

concepts_list.name: b_body_force
concepts_list.description: Body force term in the momentum equation
concepts_list.justification: Appears in the momentum equation as a bold vector term, representing external forces such as gravity.

concepts_list.name: p_pressure
concepts_list.justification: Appears in the stress tensor and in the variational form as a scalar field related to the normal stress component.

concepts_list.name: mu_viscosity
concepts_list.justification: Used in the constitutive equation for the stress tensor, appearing in the Newtonian fluid model.

concepts_list.name: T_traction
concepts_list.justification: Defined as the dot product of the normal vector and the stress tensor, appearing on boundary conditions and interface jumps.

concepts_list.name: t_time
concepts_list.description: Time variable in the governing equations
concepts_list.justification: Appears as a partial derivative with respect to time in the momentum and continuity equations.

concepts_list.name: Omega_domain
concepts_list.description: Main computational domain of the fluid system
concepts_list.justification: Repeatedly used as the domain of integration in equations (1), (2), and (16), and is the region where fluid dynamics are defined.

concepts_list.name: delta_Omega_boundary
concepts_list.justification: Appears in boundary integrals and as the interface between fluid phases, denoted as ∂Ω in the boundary term of the variational form.

concepts_list.name: q_test_function_incompressible
concepts_list.description: Test function for the incompressibility condition
concepts_list.justification: Defined in the continuity equation (17) as a test function in L^2(Ω), used to enforce the divergence-free condition.

new_concept_info.description:
Velocity field of the fluid
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears multiple times as a bold variable in momentum and continuity equations, denoted as u in the context of fluid motion.

Test function used in the variational formulation
JUSTIFICATION:
Explicitly described as a test function in H^1(Ω) that vanishes at Dirichlet boundaries, appearing in integral formulations.

Density of the fluid
JUSTIFICATION:
Used in both momentum and continuity equations, represented as ρ and associated with mass and momentum conservation.

Body force term in the momentum equation
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears in the momentum equation as a bold vector term, representing external forces such as gravity.

Pressure field in the fluid
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears in the stress tensor and in the variational form as a scalar field related to the normal stress component.

Dynamic viscosity of the fluid
JUSTIFICATION:
Used in the constitutive equation for the stress tensor, appearing in the Newtonian fluid model.

Traction vector on the boundary
JUSTIFICATION:
Defined as the dot product of the normal vector and the stress tensor, appearing on boundary conditions and interface jumps.

Time variable in the governing equations
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears as a partial derivative with respect to time in the momentum and continuity equations.

Main computational domain of the fluid system
JUSTIFICATION:
Repeatedly used as the domain of integration in equations (1), (2), and (16), and is the region where fluid dynamics are defined.

Boundary of the fluid domain
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears in boundary integrals and as the interface between fluid phases, denoted as ∂Ω in the boundary term of the variational form.
